---
title: "Vision-Language Notes"
subtitle: "Technical notes on vision-language models and their foundations"
---

These are working notes from my PhD research on vision-language models. 
My background is in physics and applied mathematics (complex systems), 
and these notes document the technical foundations I am building as I go deeper into 
computer vision, multimodal models, and representation learning.

This is not a polished textbook. It is a living document -- updated as I read, 
think, and get things wrong.

## Chapters

- [Stage 0 -- Curriculum overview](curriculum/00-overview.qmd)
- [Stage 1 -- How neural networks learn](curriculum/01-neural-networks.qmd)
- [Stage 2 -- Convolutional neural networks](curriculum/02-convolutional-neural-networks.qmd)
- [Stage 3 -- Attention and transformers](curriculum/03-attention-and-transformers.qmd)

## Background

PhD candidate in computer vision and vision-language models.
BSc Physics, MSc Modelling for Science and Engineering (complex systems track).
